(born 15 January 1939) is a Mexican former [swimmer](/wiki/Swimming_(sport) "Swimming (sport)"). From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Mexican swimmer (born 1939)

Alejandro Gaxiola

| Personal information | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | (1939-01-15) 15 January 1939 (age 87)  [Mexico City](/wiki/Mexico_City "Mexico City"), Mexico |
| Sport | |
| Sport | [Swimming](/wiki/Swimming_(sport) "Swimming (sport)") |
| Medal record  |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | | | Representing  Mexico | | | | [Pan American Games](/wiki/Pan_American_Games "Pan American Games") | | | | Bronze medal – third place | [1959 Chicago](/wiki/1959_Pan_American_Games "1959 Pan American Games") | [4x100m medley relay](/wiki/Swimming_at_the_1959_Pan_American_Games "Swimming at the 1959 Pan American Games") | | |

**Alejandro Gaxiola** (born 15 January 1939) is a Mexican former [swimmer](/wiki/Swimming_(sport) "Swimming (sport)"). He competed in [two events](/wiki/Swimming_at_the_1960_Summer_Olympics "Swimming at the 1960 Summer Olympics") at the [1960 Summer Olympics](/wiki/1960_Summer_Olympics "1960 Summer Olympics").[[1]](#cite_note-SportsRef-1)

[Sports Reference LLC](/wiki/Sports_Reference "Sports Reference"). Archived from [the original](https://www.sports-reference.com/olympics/athletes/ga/alejandro-gaxiola-1.html) on 18 April 2020. Retrieved 18 October 2016.
